name: Alaska
agency: Alaska Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing

steps: Vote to dissolve per operating agreement or majority of members
Ensure all biennial reports are current and entity is in good standing
Wind up business affairs and settle debts
Mail form with $25 filing fee to Division of Corporations
File final state tax returns with Alaska Department of Revenue
llc.notes: Entity must be current with all biennial reports and in good standing prior to voluntary dissolution. Mail filing only.

steps: Board of directors adopts resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
File Certificate of Election to Dissolve ($10 fee)
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle debts
File Articles of Dissolution (08-407) with $15 filing fee
File final state tax returns with Alaska Department of Revenue
corporation.notes: Corporation dissolution requires filing both a Certificate of Election to Dissolve ($10) and Articles of Dissolution ($15), totaling $25.

tax.notes: Alaska has no state income tax or sales tax. File final corporate income tax return if applicable. Close all state tax accounts.

requirements.employeeNotification: Follow federal WARN Act if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Reasonable time to wind up affairs
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: Majority vote of members (LLC) or shareholders (Corp) required

includes: Every required form with direct download links
